item.title: There Are Three Types of AI Users
item.description: What will differentiate people is not how smart they are but their relationship to mental effort.
item.dc:creator: David Brooks

item.title: France Is Too Hot for Shutters and Ceiling Fans
item.description: Millions of people are suffering through the hottest days of their lives with few options.
item.dc:creator: Henry Grabar

item.title: The Court That Will Believe Absolutely Anything Is ‘Race-Neutral’
item.description: The majority gives Trump yet more license to discriminate.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: Higher Ed Is Very Sorry
item.description: Universities are studying how they lost the public’s trust.
item.dc:creator: Rose Horowitch

item.title: William Barr’s Dangerous Endorsement of Todd Blanche
item.description: Trump’s former attorney general proposes a Senate blessing of Trump’s threats to the rule of law.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: The Supreme Court’s Era of Meaningless Rights
item.description: The Supreme Court is not saying people don’thavecertain rights, just that no courts can help them when those rights are violated.
item.dc:creator: Leah Litman

item.title: AI Can’t Fix the Student-Motivation Problem
item.description: It turns out bots aren’t great teachers.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Anderson

item.title: Alan Greenspan Was ‘Not Quite God’
item.description: He built an independent central bank that withstood many crises. That independence may not last forever.
item.dc:creator: Annie Lowrey

item.title: The 10,000-Year Flood
item.description: The banks of the Guadalupe River were an idyllic spot to park an RV. Then, last summer, it started to rain harder and faster than anyone could imagine.
item.dc:creator: Sean Flynn

item.title: Trump Is Making the 250th Small
item.description: The State Fair rally was an empty celebration of a man instead of a country.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols
